findings.evidence: PR lines 48-60 require `[IsProbabilityMeasure μ]` and `hmeas`; Mathlib has `mathlib/Mathlib/Probability/Independence/Basic.lean:790 lemma iIndepFun.isProbabilityMeasure (h : iIndepFun f μ) : IsProbabilityMeasure μ`, `mathlib/Mathlib/Probability/IdentDistrib.lean:74 aemeasurable_fst : AEMeasurable f μ`, and `mathlib/Mathlib/Probability/Independence/Basic.lean:840 theorem iIndepFun.map_fun_eq_pi_map ... (hf : ∀ i, AEMeasurable (f i) μ)`.
findings.fix: State the base theorem as `theorem ConditionallyIIDWith.of_iIndepFun_identDistrib {μ : Measure Ω} {X : ℕ → Ω → α} (hindep : iIndepFun X μ) (hident : ∀ i, IdentDistrib (X i) (X 0) μ μ) : ...`, using `hindep.isProbabilityMeasure` and `(hident i).aemeasurable_fst`; propagate the same weaker signature to the `ConditionallyIID`, `Exchangeable`, and `Contractable` wrappers.
findings.issue: All four `of_iIndepFun_identDistrib` theorems require `[IsProbabilityMeasure μ]` and `hmeas : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)`, but both are stronger than needed: `hindep : iIndepFun X μ` already gives `IsProbabilityMeasure μ`, and `hident` already gives `AEMeasurable` for every coordinate.

summary: The new IID API is stated with stronger hypotheses than the surrounding Mathlib/TauCeti conventions require.
